content:
# Salwa Bay

**Summary**: A highly saline area in the Arabian Gulf near the Saudi Arabia-Qatar border.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2017-amao|Amao, 2017]] investigated foraminiferal diversity and deformity rates in Salwa Bay.

## Geological and Structural Setting

- [[2017-amao|Amao, 2017]] landlocked cul-de-sac

## Formations Present

- [[2017-amao|Amao, 2017]] not specified

## Key Findings From This Location

- Salwa Bay is the most saline extension of the Arabian Gulf, with salinity reaching 77% at its southern edge. ([[2017-amao|Amao, 2017, p. 87]])
